notes: "If you will sanctify the land of Zion unto the Lord through the observance of this law, it shall be a land of Zion unto you. The Lord will bless the land and make it fruitful unto you, as He did the land of the children of Israel. In ancient times the Lord blessed the earth for those who observed the law of tithing (2 Chronicles 31:5). This law was given to the children of Israel, and when they obeyed it they were prospered and had abundance; when they disobeyed it, they were afflicted with drought, with mildew, with rust, with the devouring insect, and they were impoverished and destroyed because they did not observe the law" (Joseph F. Smith, Conference Report, April 1900, 49).

quotes.text: Through religious history we [Ensign, 32] have found how the Lord blesses the people when they reach out and care for the poor and the needy. Of the days of Hezekiah we read this in the scriptures: "And concerning the children of Israel and Judah, … they also brought in the tithe of oxen and sheep, and the tithe of holy things which were consecrated unto the Lord their God, and laid them by heaps. "And when Hezekiah and the princes came and saw the heaps, they blessed the Lord, and his people Israel. "Then Hezekiah questioned with the priests and the Levites concerning the heaps" (2 Chronicles 31:6, 8-9). The answer was, "Since the people began to bring the offerings into the house of the Lord, we have had enough to eat, and have left plenty: for the Lord hath blessed his people; and that which is left is this great store" (2 Chronicles 31:10).
quotes.speaker: L. Tom Perry
quotes.attr: L. Tom Perry, in Conference Report, Apr. 1986, 40; or Ensign, May 1986, 32

quotes.text: Hezekiah at that time commanded the people who dwelt in Jerusalem to pay their tithes and offerings. The children of Israel brought in abundance the tithe of all things and laid them by heaps, and when Hezekiah questioned the priests and the Levites concerning the heaps, Azariah, the chief priest, answered him and said, "Since the people began to bring the offerings into the house of the Lord, we have had enough to eat, and have left plenty: for the Lord hath blessed his people; and that which is left is this great store" (see 2 Chr. 31:2-10).
quotes.speaker: Marion G. Romney
quotes.attr: Marion G. Romney, Look to God and Live, p.150-151

notes: 2Ch 31:11-19. HEZEKIAH APPOINTS OFFICERS TO DISPOSE OF THE TITHES

quotes.text: In all, we have twenty-four quorums of Levites (deacons) listed here, each one having twelve members, as in all present-day quorums of deacons. (D&C 107:85.) Twelve Levites, presumably deacons, forming a quorum, are also mentioned in 2 Chronicles 31:12-13.
quotes.speaker: John A. Tvedtnes
quotes.source: The Church of the Old Testament
quotes.attr: John A. Tvedtnes, The Church of the Old Testament, p.50

quotes.text: When the blessings of the priesthood were restored at the time of Moses-through Moses-the ordinances of the priesthood were also restored. These ordinances we refer to as the works done in the flesh, which means the works done here upon this earth. These are the priesthood works. In order to do this, a portable temple was constructed. Brothers and sisters, this was only for the living. There was as yet no work for the dead. There couldn't be, for no atonement had as yet been made. The door had not yet been opened. Even in the more permanent temple of Solomon and the temple that followed after that, only work for the living was done. The priesthood ordinances were given to a select few, those who had not rejected the Melchizedek Priesthood, for most of them had rejected the Melchizedek Priesthood and its accompanying blessings and ordinances. There were a select few men and women who kept the covenants of God, who, by special permission were given the privilege of having these ordinances performed while they were living in the flesh, who were judged worthy to receive these blessings; and a genealogy was kept of the sons and daughters of God. This is referred to in the Bible and you will find it in 2 Chr. 31:16-19.
quotes.speaker: Theodore M. Burton
quotes.attr: Theodore M. Burton, August 8, 1966, BYU Speeches of the Year, 1966, p.6
